openQuestions.text: Q1: Which of impeccable's shared design laws, 23 command flows, anti-pattern rules, per-model defect blocks, and prose denylist are genuinely net-new or MORE SPECIFIC than what sk-design's five modes already encode (after the 022/023 and 024/025–027 adoptions), and which are already-covered or are build/site/test/CLI infrastructure outside sk-design's scope?

Q2: For each genuinely net-new item, which sk-design home is correct — interface, foundations, motion, audit, md-generator, the shared register, the hub, or a justified NEW mode — and what is the minimal, surgical edit (file + anchor)?

Q3: Which of impeccable's STRUCTURAL ideas (register-as-first-class, the per-model codex/gemini defect catalog, the color-strategy commitment axis, the anti-pattern detector engine, the prose denylist) are worth adopting versus ruling out as scope creep or already-analogous to sk-design's existing register / `/20` audit score / `ai_fingerprint_tells`?

Q4: What is the prioritized, concrete adoption backlog (per mode, plus any justified new-mode proposal), each item traced to an impeccable source and an sk-design target file, with leverage and effort noted, and a clear no-new-mode verdict?

carriedForwardOpenQuestions.text: Q1 also remains open for actual anti-pattern catalog semantics because the scoped detector facade did not expose rule IDs or detector descriptions.
Q4 remains open for prioritized adoption order after the remaining command-flow references are read.
Q4 remains open for final prioritized adoption order after the remaining command-flow references and detector semantics are read.
Q1 remains open for actual anti-pattern catalog semantics because the earlier detector pass only reached the scoped facade and did not expose rule IDs or detector descriptions.
Q4 remains open for final prioritized adoption order after the remaining command-flow references are read.

Remaining work is synthesis, not more corpus coverage: consolidate the adoption backlog across iterations 1-8, dedupe overlapping candidates, and prepare a future implementation packet if the user approves.
No command-flow reference remains unread from the carried-forward list.

Q2: For each genuinely net-new item, which sk-design home is correct — interface, foundations, motion, audit, md-generator, the shared register, the hub, or a justified NEW mode — and what is the minimal, surgical edit (file + anchor)?

Q3: Which of impeccable's STRUCTURAL ideas (register-as-first-class, the per-model codex/gemini defect catalog, the color-strategy commitment axis, the anti-pattern detector engine, the prose denylist) are worth adopting versus ruling out as scope creep or already-analogous to sk-design's existing register / `/20` audit score / `ai_fingerprint_tells`?

keyFindings.text: `document`/`extract` stay split between md-generator's measured Style Reference and `sk-code` implementation.
`init` reinforces the existing shared register but does not justify a PRODUCT.md mode.
`live`/`hooks` are transport and detector infrastructure.
`onboard` is a product-flow refinement inside interface/audit.
`overdrive` is a motion/interface escalation with implementation routed to `sk-code`.
`DESIGN.md` drift is an audit bridge, not md-generator authoring work.

Anti-pattern detector engine: out-of-scope infrastructure. Keep semantic rules only. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/028-impeccable-design-research/research/iterations/iteration-007.md:57]
Color-strategy commitment axis: already covered by register/foundations and earlier adoptions; no separate axis.
Copy-specificity, copy cadence, prose denylist, and localization belong in one content-gate edit.
Onboarding, destructive undo-over-confirmation, and validation timing all belong in `ux_quality_reference.md` as Product Flow Floor refinements, ordered with onboarding first.
Overlay reliability appears in iterations 1, 5, and 7. Implement once in audit hardening.
Per-model defect catalog: adopt specific tell semantics into `ai_fingerprint_tells.md`, not a separate detector architecture.

Prose denylist: adopt selected UI-copy examples into `copy_and_mock_data.md`, not STYLE's build validator, house punctuation rules, or wholesale prose police. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/028-impeccable-design-research/research/deltas/iter-003.jsonl:7]
Register-as-first-class: already covered by `sk-design/shared/register.md`; no new register system.
Typography default-font and near-duplicate font pairing can live in one foundations edit.
`design-audit`: DESIGN.md/token drift, overlay clipping, production hardening, selected AI tells.
`design-foundations` and `shared`: typography/token refinements.
`design-interface`: pre-code brief/direction, native-image palette/mocks, onboarding, content specificity, feature composition.
`design-md-generator`: remains a measured extraction source, not the owner of live UI drift.
`design-motion`: high-ambition interaction escalation.

P1: guarded native-image visual direction branch in `real_ui_loop.md`, because impeccable's Codex flow requires palette confirmation, mock approval, and ingredient inventory before code, while `sk-design` only has an optional brief-specific direction sketch. [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/codex.md:7] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/codex.md:33] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/codex.md:61] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/real_ui_loop.md:99]

P1/P2: audit refinements for live DESIGN.md drift and overlay/top-layer clipping, because current audit catches token misuse and generic overflow but not those exact live-system checks. [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/anti_patterns_production.md:32] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-audit/references/hardening_edge_cases.md:38] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/SKILL.src.md:68]

P2: first-value onboarding specifics and high-ambition motion escalation, because current product flow and motion craft cover the base but not skip/dismissal memory, contextual teaching, 2-3 extraordinary directions, and browser-verified refinement. [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/ux_quality_reference.md:81] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/onboard.md:38] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/onboard.md:211] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/overdrive.md:16] [SOURCE: .opencode/specs/design/008-sk-design-parent/external/impeccable-main/skill/reference/overdrive.md:24]

P3: copy, typography, token, and composition examples, because the current homes already cover most of the behavior and only need narrow examples. [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-interface/references/design-process/copy_and_mock_data.md:76] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/references/type/typography_system.md:80] [SOURCE: .opencode/skills/sk-design/shared/design_token_vocabulary.md:76]
